external.description: Greenland’s ice sheets contain enough fresh water to raise sea levels over twenty feet.  While this is not an immediate threat, the latest research shows that #Greenland’sGlaciers are shrinking at seven times the rate of just a few years ago, an average of 30 million tonnes an hour.   Fresh water entering the ocean from this melting has major implications for the Atlantic meridional overturning circulation , or #AMOC, which is critical to maintain global weather patterns. Peter Wadhams, Emeritus Professor of Ocean Physics at Cambridge University and Climate Scientist, Paul Beckwith describe what is happening on this sensitive continent.    Host, Dale Walkonen Editor, Mike Coe Thumbnail, Eric Dehais   For more information about #ClimateChange, visit the FacingFuture Library at https://facingfuture.earth/library.
external.title: Greenland: Ice Loss Accelerating

#Greenland: Ice Loss Accelerating

Fresh water entering the ocean from this melting has major implications for the Atlantic meridional overturning circulation , or #AMOC, which is critical to maintain global weather patterns. record.text: In good years, progress = two steps forward, one step back. In bad years, it’s one step forward, two steps back. I’d guess that we’re in the former position on #climatechange but we could too easily slip into the latter.

Weird, since transportation-wise, #Biden is merely replacing street grids with street grids — instead of expanding Amtrak and public transit like he promised to do — and mostly contributing to more #climatechange. #GOP would’ve done as much (or as little).

The real issue here is eating high-cholesterol meaty dishes five plus times a week. This way you are not only telling your health to get stuffed but also the planet and future generations! 
A plant-based diet is one of the biggest leverss we can pull to reduce our impact and tackle #Climatechange.
